pattern: Mapping each letter to grid coordinates lets a path be built by moving vertically and horizontally between consecutive targets, taking up/left before down/right so the bottom-row letter is reached legally.
hints: Each letter sits at a fixed (row, column) given by its position in the alphabet, so moving from one letter to the next is just covering the row and column difference.
The only catch is the lone bottom-row letter: enter its column before dropping into the last row, and leave that row before changing column.
For each target letter, emit all 'U'/'L' moves first and all 'D'/'R' moves after, then a '!', which both minimizes moves and keeps you on the board for that bottom cell.

edge_inputs.description: single first letter requires no movement
the bottom-row letter must be entered and exited carefully
moving to and away from z mid-word
repeated letter needs no movement between repeats

stress.description: random lowercase strings up to the length bound
stress.note: includes z frequently to exercise the bottom-row handling
